$comment: Documented-EXCEPTION ledger for the parity closed loop. SOURCE OF TRUTH = the shots + specs generated from the mock design; any visual divergence from them is a BUG to FIX by default. This ledger holds ONLY the narrow, documented exceptions where the FE intentionally does NOT match the mock, each governed by a doc — it is NOT a 'redesign' escape hatch. Valid exception kinds: 'behavior' (docs/business win behaviour over the mock's visual — e.g. study flip-card), 'future'/'rejected' (feature scope per docs), 'needs-schema' (data not in the read model yet). A finding that MATCHES an entry → exception (cite source); anything not listed → FIX. Match: screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.kind == '*' OR entry.kind == finding kind). source MUST cite a doc/owner ruling. Keep this MINIMAL — when in doubt, leave it out and fix the FE to match the mock.

$example.reason: FE uses swipe/both-sides per study-flow; the kit flip-card is a documented behaviour-owned visual gap, not a fix.
